Digital Banking Services.Internet Banking (IB): Retail and corporate internet banking portal operations, customer authentication UX, and phishing awareness. Excludes: backend cybersecurity controls, network architecture, and security policy.
Digital Banking Services.Mobile Banking (MB): Customer-facing mobile banking app features, UX, mobile OTP, and app release management. Excludes: mobile device security controls, network security, or endpoint security policy.
Digital Banking Services.Digital Lending: Digital loan origination, servicing, and credit decisioning platforms. Excludes: regulatory filings.
Digital Banking Services.API Banking / Open Banking: Open APIs, fintech integrations, and third-party developer access. Excludes: internal network security.

Cybersecurity Wing.Security Operations Center (SOC): Real-time threat monitoring, SIEM, incident detection and response, cyber drills, IB-CART reporting. Excludes: regulatory compliance filings.
Cybersecurity Wing.Vulnerability Management: VA/PT exercises, patch management, malware and antivirus controls, remediation tracking. Excludes: managing third-party vendor contracts.
Cybersecurity Wing.Identity & Access Management: Authentication systems, MFA, privileged access management, password policies, dormant account controls. Excludes: fraud risk assessment.
Cybersecurity Wing.Security Architecture: Secure system design, data classification, encryption policy, network segmentation, security baseline configuration. Excludes: day-to-day incident monitoring.

IT Vertical.Infrastructure Management: Physical and logical IT infrastructure: servers, network devices, device inventory, physical security, wired/wireless networks. Excludes: cybersecurity policy and threat response.
IT Vertical.Cloud Operations: Cloud service provisioning, hosted infrastructure, SaaS platform operations. Excludes: on-premise server management.
IT Vertical.Application Management: Core banking applications, SDLC security, software patch tracking, email servers, endpoint software controls. Excludes: network device configuration.
IT Vertical.Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery: BCP/DR planning, RTO/RPO objectives, cyber crisis management plan (CCMP), resilience testing. Excludes: real-time incident detection.

Procurement & Vendor Management.IT Procurement: Technology hardware and software sourcing, vendor selection, and procurement contracts. Excludes: ongoing vendor risk monitoring.
Procurement & Vendor Management.Third-Party Risk Management: Vendor due diligence, outsourcing risk assessment, third-party personnel vetting, oversight of service providers. Excludes: internal IT operations.
Procurement & Vendor Management.Cloud Vendor Management: Cloud provider governance, SLA management, and cloud outsourcing oversight. Excludes: internet banking and mobile banking operations.

Credit Card Vertical.Card Issuance: Card onboarding, activation, and new card product management. Excludes: transaction dispute resolution.
Credit Card Vertical.Card Operations: Card transaction processing, billing cycles, and card network operations. Excludes: fraud chargebacks.
Credit Card Vertical.Dispute & Chargeback Management: Customer dispute resolution, chargeback processing, and transaction reversals. Excludes: fraud prevention systems.

Payments Vertical.UPI: UPI transaction processing, NPCI limit compliance, and merchant UPI reconciliation. Excludes: card-based payments.
Payments Vertical.NEFT: National Electronic Funds Transfer batch processing and settlement. Excludes: real-time gross settlement.
Payments Vertical.RTGS: Real-time gross settlement of high-value interbank transactions. Excludes: retail batch transfers.
Payments Vertical.Merchant Acquiring: Merchant onboarding, POS acceptance infrastructure, and merchant settlement. Excludes: consumer card issuance.

Compliance Department.Regulatory Compliance: Statutory RBI filings, board-level regulatory reporting, KYC compliance monitoring, and circular board noting. Excludes: cybersecurity controls, IT infrastructure, vendor management, and audit execution.
Compliance Department.AML/CFT: Anti-money laundering transaction screening, sanctions list checks, and suspicious transaction reporting to FIU-IND. Excludes: general regulatory filings.

Legal Department.Contract Management: Vendor and service provider contracts, audit-right clauses, and regulatory inspection provisions. Excludes: ongoing vendor risk monitoring.
Legal Department.Privacy & Data Protection: Regulatory data privacy obligations, consent management, and data localisation law compliance. Excludes: technical implementation of confidentiality, integrity, or availability controls.

Risk Management.Operational Risk: Operational risk assessment, fraud risk monitoring, transaction surveillance, and business process risk controls. Excludes: cybersecurity policy governance.
Risk Management.Technology Risk: Board-level cyber and technology risk governance, risk categorisation, KRI/KPI frameworks, and risk appetite setting. Excludes: hands-on cybersecurity implementation.

Internal Audit.Information Systems Audit: Independent IT and cybersecurity audits, audit log review, VA/PT findings oversight, and forensic readiness assurance. Excludes: implementing security controls.
Internal Audit.Regulatory Audit: Compliance assurance testing, regulatory adherence audits, and internal control testing. Excludes: IT systems auditing.

rules.action: Ensure that UPI transactions are processed within NPIC limits and daily reconciliation is done.
rules.reasoning: Standard UPI operations and daily merchant reconciliations belong to Payments Vertical.

rules.action: Submit the annual AML / CFT assessment report to the Financial Intelligence Unit.
rules.reasoning: Filing AML/CFT reports is a core compliance and regulatory reporting duty.

rules.action: Set up a Security Operations Centre to monitor and manage cyber risks in real time.
rules.reasoning: SOC setup and real-time cyber risk monitoring is the core mandate of the SOC sub-vertical.

rules.action: Ensure continuous surveillance and keep the bank updated on the latest emerging cyber threats.
rules.reasoning: Continuous threat surveillance and intelligence feeds are primary SOC responsibilities.

rules.action: Establish systems to collect and share threat information from local, national, and international sources.
rules.reasoning: Threat intelligence collection and sharing is a core SOC function.

rules.action: Define incident response procedures, allocate roles, and periodically test the incident response plan.
rules.reasoning: Incident response planning and testing is owned by the SOC.

rules.action: Actively participate in cyber drills conducted under the aegis of CERT-In and IDRBT.
rules.reasoning: Cyber drill participation and coordination with CERT-In is a SOC responsibility.

rules.action: Conduct periodic vulnerability assessment and penetration testing of all internet-facing systems.
rules.reasoning: VA/PT exercises are owned by the Vulnerability Management sub-vertical.

rules.action: Monitor the release of security patches from vendors and apply them expeditiously per patch management policy.
rules.reasoning: Patch management and timely remediation of known vulnerabilities is Vulnerability Management.

rules.action: Implement anti-malware and antivirus protection with behavioural detection across all endpoint devices.
rules.reasoning: Malware defence and antivirus tooling falls under Vulnerability Management.

rules.action: Implement centralised multi-factor authentication for access to all critical banking systems.
rules.reasoning: MFA and centralised authentication are Identity & Access Management controls.

rules.action: Disallow administrative rights on end-user workstations and enforce least-privilege access principles.
rules.reasoning: Least-privilege and admin rights controls are Identity & Access Management.

rules.action: Implement controls to log and monitor privileged and superuser access to critical servers and databases.
rules.reasoning: Privileged access management and audit logging of admin sessions is IAM.

rules.action: Design IT architecture to incorporate security controls and network segmentation at all times.
rules.reasoning: Secure IT architecture design and network segmentation is Security Architecture.

rules.action: Develop a data loss prevention strategy and classify data based on sensitivity and confidentiality.
rules.reasoning: Data classification and DLP strategy belong to Security Architecture.

rules.action: Maintain an up-to-date centralised inventory of all authorised hardware, software, and network devices.
rules.reasoning: Asset inventory management is an IT Infrastructure Management responsibility.

rules.action: Put in place physical security measures and environmental controls for the bank's critical IT assets.
rules.reasoning: Physical security and environmental controls for data centres are IT Infrastructure Management.

rules.action: Prepare and maintain an up-to-date network architecture diagram including all wired and wireless networks.
rules.reasoning: Network topology documentation is an IT Infrastructure Management task.

rules.action: Incorporate information security controls across all stages of the application development lifecycle.
rules.reasoning: Secure SDLC practices are owned by Application Management.

rules.action: Manage changes to core banking applications using robust configuration management processes.
rules.reasoning: Application change management and configuration baseline is Application Management.

rules.action: Ensure BCP and DR capabilities meet the bank's cyber resilience objectives and recovery time targets.
rules.reasoning: BCP/DR planning and testing against RTO objectives is this sub-vertical's mandate.

rules.action: Develop a Cyber Crisis Management Plan and integrate it into the Board-approved strategy.
rules.reasoning: CCMP development is a BC/DR responsibility linked to cyber resilience.

rules.action: Develop a Board-approved cyber risk framework with risk categorisation and key risk indicators.
rules.reasoning: Cyber risk governance frameworks and KRIs are Technology Risk responsibilities.

rules.action: Identify and assess inherent risks in technologies adopted, delivery channels, and internal threats.
rules.reasoning: Inherent technology risk identification is a Technology Risk function.

rules.action: Ensure Board-level oversight and involvement in setting the right tone on cybersecurity governance.
rules.reasoning: Board-level cyber governance and risk appetite setting is Technology Risk.

rules.action: Implement risk-based transaction monitoring across all delivery channels for fraud risk management.
rules.reasoning: Fraud risk transaction monitoring is an Operational Risk control.

rules.action: Report all cybersecurity incidents to the Reserve Bank of India in the prescribed format.
rules.reasoning: Incident reporting to the RBI is a regulatory compliance filing obligation.

rules.action: Place a copy of the RBI circular before the Board of Directors in its ensuing meeting.
rules.reasoning: Board circulation of RBI directives is a regulatory compliance formality.

rules.action: Manage and analyse audit logs systematically to detect, understand, and recover from cyber attacks.
rules.reasoning: Audit log management and forensic readiness are Information Systems Audit responsibilities.

rules.action: Independently assess adherence to the cyber resilience framework through audits by qualified professionals.
rules.reasoning: Independent compliance audits of IT/cyber controls are Information Systems Audit.

rules.action: Conduct effective due diligence and risk assessment before outsourcing critical processes to third-party vendors.
rules.reasoning: Vendor due diligence and outsourcing risk assessment is Third-Party Risk Management.

rules.action: Mandate background checks and non-disclosure agreements for all third-party service provider personnel.
rules.reasoning: Personnel vetting for third parties is a Third-Party Risk Management control.

rules.action: Enter into an agreement with service providers that includes right of audit by the bank and regulatory inspection.
rules.reasoning: Audit rights and inspection clauses in vendor contracts are a Contract Management obligation.

rules.action: Adhere to legal requirements on data localisation and movement of customer data across national borders.
rules.reasoning: Data localisation and cross-border data transfer rules are Privacy & Data Protection.

rules.action: Educate customers on the risks of sharing login credentials with third-party vendors.
rules.reasoning: Customer security education for online banking credentials is an Internet Banking responsibility.

rules.action: Encourage customers to report phishing mails and phishing sites and take effective remedial action.
rules.reasoning: Anti-phishing customer communication is an Internet Banking (IB) function.

rules.action: Ensure that UPI transactions are processed within NPCI daily limits and reconciliation is completed.
rules.reasoning: UPI transaction processing and daily reconciliation is a Payments Vertical / UPI function.
